title: Entertainment Behavior Under Academic Pressure
subtitle: A public data story from YouTube, Spotify, Netflix, Prime Video, and the academic calendar.

datasets.name: YouTube
datasets.notes: Google Takeout activity converted into a public activity table. Channel names are masked.

datasets.name: Spotify
datasets.notes: Music-focused streaming history with exact timestamps and listening duration.

datasets.name: Netflix
datasets.notes: Viewing history kept at row level. Titles stay visible; local source paths are not exposed.

datasets.name: Prime Video
datasets.notes: Watch history parsed into movie and episode records where possible.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Activity Count By Action
edaPlots.caption: YouTube is dominated by watch activity, while searches are a smaller but useful secondary behavior.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Activity Count By Hour
edaPlots.caption: Activity clusters in the evening and late night, supporting the after-21:30 analysis window.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Activity Count By Target Kind
edaPlots.caption: Most rows target videos, so YouTube is treated mainly as a watch-history signal.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Daily After 2130 Watched Count
edaPlots.caption: Late-night watch counts are bursty, with large spikes in the later part of the timeline.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Daily Watched And Search Counts
edaPlots.caption: Watched counts are much larger than search counts, and daily behavior is highly spiky.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Daily Watched By Weekday Boxplot
edaPlots.caption: Weekday medians are fairly close, while outliers appear on every weekday.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Daily Watched Search Distribution
edaPlots.caption: Daily watch and search counts are right-skewed, so nonparametric tests are safer.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Daily Watched Vs Search Scatter
edaPlots.caption: Search tends to rise on heavier YouTube days, but watch activity is not driven only by search.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Eda Overview Panel
edaPlots.caption: A compact YouTube summary: spiky daily counts, late-night concentration, and smaller search behavior.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Monthly After 2130 Share
edaPlots.caption: The late-night share varies strongly by month and should be read together with total volume.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Monthly Watched And Search Counts
edaPlots.caption: Monthly YouTube activity has visible peaks, especially around late 2024 and late 2025.

edaPlots.title: Youtube Top Masked Channel Refs
edaPlots.caption: A small set of masked channel references accounts for repeated activity, without revealing channel names.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Daily After 2130 Hours
edaPlots.caption: After-21:30 listening appears throughout the timeline and has repeated high-listening spikes.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Daily Hours By Weekday Boxplot
edaPlots.caption: Spotify hours exist across all weekdays, with slightly higher weekend medians and many outliers.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Daily Hours Distribution
edaPlots.caption: Spotify daily hours are right-skewed, with many low-listening days and a few very high days.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Daily Listening Hours
edaPlots.caption: Daily Spotify listening becomes more consistent after 2021 and rises strongly across 2024-2025.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Daily Streams Vs Hours
edaPlots.caption: Daily stream count and listening hours move together strongly, but not perfectly.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Eda Overview Panel
edaPlots.caption: A compact Spotify summary: rising usage, skewed hours, strong stream-hour relation, and time-of-day structure.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Listening Hours By Hour
edaPlots.caption: Spotify has a daytime block around late morning and a second evening/night block.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Monthly Listening Hours
edaPlots.caption: Monthly listening peaks in 2025, suggesting a longer-term habit shift beyond academic timing alone.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Top 15 Artists By Hours
edaPlots.caption: Listening is concentrated around a few artists, but content preference is not part of the main tests.

edaPlots.title: Spotify Top 15 Tracks By Hours
edaPlots.caption: Top tracks show repeated listening, but daily hours remain the cleaner testing variable.

edaPlots.title: Combined Long Form Distribution
edaPlots.caption: Netflix + Prime daily viewing is zero-heavy and bursty rather than a daily habit.

edaPlots.title: Combined Long Form Streaming Daily Count
edaPlots.caption: Long-form viewing appears in bursts, including a large Prime Video-driven spike in 2024.

edaPlots.title: Daily Count Distribution Active Days
edaPlots.caption: On active days, Netflix is generally more frequent than Prime Video, but both are bursty.

edaPlots.title: Daily Viewing Count Netflix Vs Prime
edaPlots.caption: Netflix and Prime Video spikes often appear in different periods, so they are grouped only at the EDA layer.

edaPlots.title: Long Form Daily Count By Weekday Boxplot
edaPlots.caption: Weekday medians are zero, so weekday is not a strong long-form streaming explanation.

edaPlots.title: Long Form Streaming By Platform
edaPlots.caption: Netflix is the more regular long-form platform, while Prime Video appears in shorter bursts.

edaPlots.title: Monthly Combined Long Form Count
edaPlots.caption: Monthly long-form streaming has strong peaks, especially around 2025-01.

edaPlots.title: Monthly Long Form Count By Platform
edaPlots.caption: Netflix drives most large monthly peaks, while Prime Video contributes isolated bursts.

edaPlots.title: Netflix Title Quality Split
edaPlots.caption: Most Netflix titles are usable; malformed title rows are a small minority.

edaPlots.title: Netflix Vs Prime Daily Scatter
edaPlots.caption: Most active days belong to one service or the other, so same-day high usage is rare.

edaPlots.title: Prime Netflix Eda Overview Panel
edaPlots.caption: A compact long-form summary: sparse daily counts, weak same-day platform overlap, and episode-heavy Prime Video.

edaPlots.title: Prime Video Record Type Split
edaPlots.caption: Prime Video is mostly episode watching, with movies as a much smaller subset.

edaPlots.title: Top 15 Netflix Title Groups
edaPlots.caption: Netflix viewing is concentrated in repeated series such as How I Met Your Mother and Friends.

edaPlots.title: Top 15 Prime Video Title Groups
edaPlots.caption: Prime Video is also series-heavy, led by Supernatural and Game of Thrones.

edaPlots.title: Total Long Form Count By Platform Common Range
edaPlots.caption: Netflix contributes more long-form rows than Prime Video in the common comparison range.

edaPlots.title: Combined Active Days By Platform
edaPlots.caption: Spotify is the most continuous platform, while Netflix and Prime Video are sparse.

edaPlots.title: Combined After 2130 By Analysis Period Boxplots
edaPlots.caption: Late-night YouTube and Spotify activity are lower during finals in the current basic tests.

edaPlots.title: Combined Daily Distribution Overview
edaPlots.caption: All main daily variables are skewed, especially YouTube counts and Netflix + Prime counts.

edaPlots.title: Combined Daily Variable Correlation Heatmap
edaPlots.caption: YouTube variables correlate with each other; Spotify variables correlate with each other; long-form viewing is weakly tied to both.

edaPlots.title: Combined Dataset Date Coverage
edaPlots.caption: The common comparison window is where all four public platform datasets overlap.

edaPlots.title: Combined Eda Overview Panel
edaPlots.caption: A compact cross-platform overview of monthly trends, platform diversity, and Spotify-YouTube scatter.

edaPlots.title: Combined Monthly After 2130 Activity
edaPlots.caption: Late-night YouTube and Spotify peaks happen in different months, so they are related but separate behaviors.

edaPlots.title: Combined Monthly Cross Platform Trends
edaPlots.caption: YouTube, Spotify, and Netflix + Prime peak in different months, showing different entertainment rhythms.

edaPlots.title: Combined Netflix Prime Vs Youtube Watched Scatter
edaPlots.caption: Netflix + Prime days do not show a strong same-day relationship with YouTube watched count.

edaPlots.title: Combined Platform Activity By Analysis Period Boxplots
edaPlots.caption: Each platform metric is shown separately because the units differ across services.

edaPlots.title: Combined Platform Diversity By Analysis Period
edaPlots.caption: Platform diversity is usually one or two active platforms per day, with modest period differences.

edaPlots.title: Combined Relative Activity By Analysis Period
edaPlots.caption: Relative scaling shows ordinary term above usual YouTube activity and summer work above usual Spotify hours.

edaPlots.title: Combined Spotify Hours Vs Youtube Watched Scatter
edaPlots.caption: Spotify hours and YouTube watched count show a weak positive relationship rather than a strong linear pattern.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H1 Entertainment usage during academic pressure
hypothesisResults.interpretation: Current basic result does not provide strong evidence that this platform is lower during finals.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H1 Entertainment usage during academic pressure
hypothesisResults.interpretation: Current basic result does not provide strong evidence that this platform is lower during finals.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H1 Entertainment usage during academic pressure
hypothesisResults.interpretation: Current basic result does not provide strong evidence that this platform is lower during finals.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H1 Entertainment usage during academic pressure
hypothesisResults.interpretation: Current basic result does not provide strong evidence that this platform is lower during finals.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H2 Platform diversity during academic pressure
hypothesisResults.interpretation: Platform diversity is slightly lower in finals, but the current basic test does not reject H0.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H3 Long-form streaming and YouTube activity
hypothesisResults.interpretation: The current basic test does not support lower YouTube activity on Netflix+Prime active days.

hypothesisResults.outcome: Spotify hours and YouTube watched count
hypothesisResults.interpretation: The association is statistically detectable in this basic test, but the effect is weak.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H5 After-9:30 PM entertainment during finals
hypothesisResults.interpretation: The YouTube late-evening share is lower during finals in the current basic test.

hypothesisResults.hypothesis: H5 After-9:30 PM entertainment during finals
hypothesisResults.interpretation: The Spotify late-evening listening share is lower during finals in the current basic test.

roadmap: Polish formal hypothesis-test interpretation for the report.
Add multiple-testing correction only if the project needs a stricter statistical layer.
Add machine-learning cards later only when a real model exists.
